title: Diversity Empowers Intelligence: Integrating Expertise of Software Engineering Agents
reason: LLM agent framework for software engineering, directly improves code task success via multi-agent consensus.

title: OpenHands: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents
reason: Infrastructure for AI agents that write code and solve software engineering tasks (SWE-Bench). Directly aligns with LLM-based agents for code.

title: Agent Skill Acquisition for Large Language Models via CycleQD
reason: Method for LLM skill acquisition improves agent performance on coding tasks in AgentBench.

title: SWE-Search: Enhancing Software Agents with Monte Carlo Tree Search and Iterative Refinement
reason: LLM software agent using MCTS for iterative refinement and self-improvement on code tasks (SWE-bench).

title: Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Collaboration Networks for Software Development
reason: Self-evolving LLM multi-agent system for software development, directly addressing code generation and correctness from requirements.

title: Internet of Agents: Weaving a Web of Heterogeneous Agents for Collaborative Intelligence
reason: LLM-based multi-agent framework for collaboration; validates on tasks requiring reasoning, relevant for agent workflows for code.

title: ToolGen: Unified Tool Retrieval and Calling via Generation
reason: ToolGen integrates tool calling directly into LLM generation, a strong signal for LLM agents in programming tasks.

title: Execution-guided within-prompt search for programming-by-example
reason: LLM-guided search using program execution results to synthesize code, combining syntactic and semantic reasoning.

title: Learning to Plan Before Answering: Self-Teaching LLMs to Learn Abstract Plans for Problem Solving
reason: LLM self-training for abstract plans, aligns with structured intermediate reasoning for code.

title: AgentRefine: Enhancing Agent Generalization through Refinement Tuning
reason: LLM agent framework with self-refinement for error correction, directly applicable to code debugging and program repair.

title: Planning in Natural Language Improves LLM Search for Code Generation
reason: Proposes PlanSearch, a method using natural language planning for diverse code generation, directly improving LLM reliability for programming.

title: AFlow: Automating Agentic Workflow Generation
reason: Automates LLM agent workflow generation for code, using search and execution feedback.

title: Language Agents Meet Causality -- Bridging LLMs and Causal World Models
reason: Integrates causal world models with LLMs for planning/reasoning, aligning with structured reasoning themes.

title: MindSearch: Mimicking Human Minds Elicits Deep AI Searcher
reason: LLM-based multi-agent system for complex information seeking, parallels multi-step reasoning for code tasks.

title: Learning Evolving Tools for Large Language Models
reason: Tool learning framework (ToolEVO) for LLMs in dynamic environments, using MCTS for self-reflection and code tool updates.

title: Learning to Communicate Through Implicit Communication Channels
reason: Multi-agent implicit communication for collaborative tasks; relevant to agent coordination for programming workflows.

title: Visual-O1: Understanding Ambiguous Instructions via Multi-modal Multi-turn Chain-of-thoughts Reasoning
reason: Addresses ambiguity in instructions via reasoning, relevant to our theme of ambiguity detection/repair in programming specs.

title: Automated Design of Agentic Systems
reason: Automates design of code-based LLM agents, validated on coding tasks; aligns with agentic reasoning for programming.

title: Flow: Modularized Agentic Workflow Automation
reason: LLM-based multi-agent framework for automated task execution with dynamic workflow refinement.

title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents
reason: LLM agent for text-based games using Monte Carlo planning; combines reasoning with search for decision-making.

title: Do LLM Agents Have Regret? A Case Study in Online Learning and Games
reason: Studies LLM agent decision-making in repeated games, quantifying performance via regret, relevant to agent reliability.

title: Facilitating Multi-turn Function Calling for LLMs via Compositional Instruction Tuning
reason: Method for multi-turn LLM function calling for compositional tasks; relates to tool-using agents for complex planning.

title: AgentOccam: A Simple Yet Strong Baseline for LLM-Based Web Agents
reason: LLM-based web agent with focus on grounding agent actions/observations for reliability.

title: Visual Agents as Fast and Slow Thinkers
reason: Proposes agent architecture with dual reasoning modes (System 1/2) for visual tasks, potentially transferable to code reasoning.

title: OS-ATLAS: Foundation Action Model for Generalist GUI Agents
reason: GUI agent framework involves executable language grounding, connecting to tool-using agents for software tasks.

title: Web Agents with World Models: Learning and Leveraging Environment Dynamics in Web Navigation
reason: LLM web agent uses world model for planning; relates to tool-using agents and reasoning about action outcomes.

title: AgentSquare: Automatic LLM Agent Search in Modular Design Space
reason: Automatic search for LLM agent architectures, including planning, reasoning, and tool use modules for diverse tasks.

title: LLM-SR: Scientific Equation Discovery via Programming with Large Language Models
reason: LLM programming for symbolic equation discovery, combines code generation with domain reasoning

title: Navigating the Digital World as Humans Do: Universal Visual Grounding for GUI Agents
reason: GUI agents using MLLMs for pixel-level reasoning; connects agents to software interfaces.

title: Language Guided Skill Discovery
reason: Uses LLMs to guide skill discovery for code-like tasks (robot control), aligning with LLM-based reasoning for structured behavior.

title: RepoGraph: Enhancing AI Software Engineering with Repository-level Code Graph
reason: Enhances LLM-based software engineering with repository-level code graphs for context, improving performance on SWE-bench.

title: Tool-Planner: Task Planning with Clusters across Multiple Tools
reason: Presents an LLM agent framework (Tool-Planner) for task planning with toolkits, directly addressing tool-using agents for code/tasks.

title: CodePlan: Unlocking Reasoning Potential in Large Language Models by Scaling Code-form Planning
reason: Uses code-form plans for structured reasoning in LLMs, aligning with structured intermediate representations for multi-step tasks.

reason: LLM agents for exploration; uses foundation model reasoning for state selection, transferable to code reasoning.

reason: Benchmarks LLM code generation with complex instructions and tool use (139 libraries), evaluating reliability.
